---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
load("C:/Users/Liam/Desktop/Lund/Courses/non-linear time ser/ex3/Exercise3.RData")
library(ctsmr)
```



```{r}

sdeTiTm <- function(data, yTi,Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph <- Ph
  # Generate a new object of class ctsm
  model = ctsm()
  # Add a system equation and thereby also a state
  # Gv in Ti: Aw/Ci*Gv or Tm: Aw/Cm*Gv
  model$addSystem(dTi ~  1/Ci*(1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 1/Rim*(Tm-Ti)  + Ph + Aw*Gv)*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~  1/Cm*(1/Rim*(Ti-Tm))*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)
  # Set the names of the inputs
  model$addInput(Ta,Gv,Ph)
  # Set the observation equation: Ti is the state, yTi is the measured output
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  # Set the variance of the measurement error
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Set the initial value (for the optimization) of the value of the state at the starting time point
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Set the initial value for the optimization
  model$setParameter(Ci = c(init = 1, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm = c(init = 1000, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 20, lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20, lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Aw = c(init = 6, lb = 1E-2, ub = 7.5+4.8+5))
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1, lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1, lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------    

  # Run the parameter optimization

  fit = model$estimate(data,firstorder = TRUE)
  return(fit)
}
```

```{r}
#this is the original estimate of room3
fit1 <- sdeTiTm(AllDat,AllDat$yTi1,AllDat$Ph1)

summary(fit1,extended=TRUE)

Hour <- as.numeric(strftime(AllDat$date, format="%H"))
Pred1 <- predict(fit1)
residuals <- Pred1[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with constant Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```


```{r}
library(splines)
idx <- (Hour>8 & Hour < 23) # It is impossible to fit a window area for the hours without any sun, so we limit the window area estimation to the hours with sun.
bs = bs(Hour[idx],df=5,intercept=TRUE) 

# What does the splines look like?
plot(bs[14:27,1],type='l')
lines(bs[ 14:27,2])
lines(bs[ 14:27,3])
lines(bs[ 14:27,4])
lines(bs[ 14:27,5])

bs1 <- bs2 <- bs3 <- bs4 <- bs5 <- bs6 <- numeric(dim(AllDat)[1])

bs1[idx] = bs[,1]
bs2[idx] = bs[,2]
bs3[idx] = bs[,3]
bs4[idx] = bs[,4]
bs5[idx] = bs[,5]

AllDat$bs1 = bs1
AllDat$bs2 = bs2
AllDat$bs3 = bs3
AllDat$bs4 = bs4
AllDat$bs5 = bs5
```



```{r}
#To build a new function
sdeTiTm2 <- function(data, yTi,Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph <- Ph
  # Generate a new object of class ctsm
  model = ctsm()
  # Add a system equation and thereby also a state
  # Gv in Ti: Aw/Ci*Gv or Tm: Aw/Cm*Gv
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + Ph + 
                  (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5)*Gv )*dt + 
                  exp(p11)*dw1)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~  1/Cm*(1/Rim*(Ti-Tm))*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)
  # Set the names of the inputs
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5)
  # Set the observation equation: Ti is the state, yTi is the measured output
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  # Set the variance of the measurement error
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Set the initial value (for the optimization) of the value of the state at the starting time point
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Set the initial value for the optimization
  model$setParameter(Ci = c(init = 1, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm = c(init = 1000, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 20, lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20, lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))

  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1, lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1, lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=100))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=100))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=100))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=100))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=100))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------    

  # Run the parameter optimization

  fit = model$estimate(data,firstorder = TRUE)
  return(fit)
}
```

```{r}
fit2 <- sdeTiTm2(AllDat,AllDat$yTi1,AllDat$Ph1)
```

```{r}
summary(fit2,extended=TRUE)
plot(bs[14:27,1]*fit2$xm["Aw1"] +   
     bs[14:27,2]*fit2$xm["Aw2"] + 
     bs[14:27,3]*fit2$xm["Aw3"] + 
     bs[14:27,4]*fit2$xm["Aw4"] + 
     bs[14:27,5]*fit2$xm["Aw5"], 
     type='l', main="Effective Window Area over Time")
```


```{r}
# 1. Generate predictions
Pred2 <- predict(fit2) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit2 <- Pred2[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit2 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```

```{r}
rmse <- sqrt(mean(residuals_fit2^2, na.rm = TRUE))

# Compute MAE (Mean Absolute Error) - reflects average deviation
mae <- mean(abs(residuals_fit2), na.rm = TRUE)

# ========================================================
# 6. Model selection metrics (AIC, BIC)
# ========================================================

# Extract information from the fit10 object
nll <- fit2$loglik      # Negative Log-Likelihood
k   <- length(fit2$xm)  # Number of model parameters
n   <- length(na.omit(residuals_fit2)) # Number of effective observations

# Compute AIC and BIC (lower is better)
aic_val <- 2 * k + 2 * nll
bic_val <- log(n) * k + 2 * nll

# ========================================================
# 7. Print evaluation report
# ========================================================
cat("\n--------------------------------------------\n")
cat("       Model fit2 Evaluation Report       \n")
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
cat(sprintf("RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error): %.4f °C\n", rmse))
cat(sprintf("MAE  (Mean Absolute Error)    : %.4f °C\n", mae))
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
cat(sprintf("AIC  (Akaike Information Criterion)  : %.2f\n", aic_val))
cat(sprintf("BIC  (Bayesian Information Criterion): %.2f\n", bic_val))
cat(sprintf("LogLikelihood                  : %.2f\n", -nll)) # Note: LogLikelihood is often displayed as a negative number
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")

# ========================================================
# 8. Autocorrelation plot (ACF) - check for remaining structure
# ========================================================
# Open a new window or plot below the original figure
# If you want to see residuals and ACF in the same figure, uncomment the line below
# par(mfrow=c(2,1)) 

# Plot ACF
acf(residuals_fit2, 
    lag.max = 24, # Check correlation over the past 24 hours
    na.action = na.pass, 
    main = "ACF of Residuals (fit2)")
```




Now try setting bs to 8 and the time window from 8 to 10pm.

```{r}
library(splines)
Hour <- as.numeric(strftime(AllDat$date, format="%H"))
# 1. Modify time window: 8:00 to 22:00 (including 22)
idx <- (Hour >= 8 & Hour <= 22) 

# 2. Modify number of splines: set df to 8
bs = bs(Hour[idx], df=8, intercept=TRUE) 

# --- Visualization check (optional) ---
# Since we have 8 splines, we can plot a subset to inspect their shapes.
# Note: just ensure the indices are within bounds.
plot_idx <- 14:40 # Take an arbitrary consecutive slice
if(max(plot_idx) <= nrow(bs)) {
  plot(bs[plot_idx, 1], type='l', ylim=c(0,1), main="Visualizing 8 Splines", ylab="Value")
  for(i in 2:8) { lines(bs[plot_idx, i], col=i) }
}

# 3. Initialize 8 all-zero vectors (note the added bs6, bs7, bs8)
bs1 <- bs2 <- bs3 <- bs4 <- bs5 <- bs6 <- bs7 <- bs8 <- numeric(dim(AllDat)[1])

# 4. Fill in the computed spline values
bs1[idx] = bs[,1]
bs2[idx] = bs[,2]
bs3[idx] = bs[,3]
bs4[idx] = bs[,4]
bs5[idx] = bs[,5]
bs6[idx] = bs[,6] # added
bs7[idx] = bs[,7] # added
bs8[idx] = bs[,8] # added

# 5. Write the new variables into the dataset AllDat
AllDat$bs1 = bs1
AllDat$bs2 = bs2
AllDat$bs3 = bs3
AllDat$bs4 = bs4
AllDat$bs5 = bs5
AllDat$bs6 = bs6 # added
AllDat$bs7 = bs7 # added
AllDat$bs8 = bs8 # added

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# ⚠️ Next steps (verbal reminder; you need to edit your function file):
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# In your sdeTiTmAv function, you need to make the following changes:
# 1. model$addSystem(dTi ~ ... + (Aw1*bs1 + ... + Aw8*bs8)*Gv ...)
# 2. model$addInput(..., bs6, bs7, bs8)
# 3. model$setParameter(Aw6 = ...), Aw7 = ..., Aw8 = ...)
```

This is the 8-spline version.

```{r}
sdeTiTm3 <- function(data, yTi,Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph <- Ph
  # Generate a new object of class ctsm
  model = ctsm()
  # Add a system equation and thereby also a state
  # Gv in Ti: Aw/Ci*Gv or Tm: Aw/Cm*Gv
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + Ph + 
                  (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5+ Aw6*bs6+ Aw7*bs7+ Aw8*bs8)*Gv )*dt + 
                  exp(p11)*dw1)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~  1/Cm*(1/Rim*(Ti-Tm))*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)
  # Set the names of the inputs
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5,bs6,bs7,bs8)
  # Set the observation equation: Ti is the state, yTi is the measured output
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  # Set the variance of the measurement error
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Set the initial value (for the optimization) of the value of the state at the starting time point
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Set the initial value for the optimization
  model$setParameter(Ci = c(init = 1, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm = c(init = 1000, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 20, lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20, lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))

  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1, lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1, lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw6 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw7 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw8 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  ##----------------------------------------------------------------    

  # Run the parameter optimization

  fit = model$estimate(data,firstorder = TRUE)
  return(fit)
}

```


```{r}
fit3 <- sdeTiTm3(AllDat,AllDat$yTi1,AllDat$Ph1)
summary(fit3,extended=TRUE)
# 1. Decide which segment to plot (using the first day is sufficient)
# Your time window is 8:00 - 22:00, totaling 15 hours
```

```{r}
# 1. Extract all valid hours within the time window
all_valid_hours <- Hour[idx]

# 2. Find the first occurrence of "8 o'clock"
# This marks the start of a day's morning window
start_index <- which(all_valid_hours == 8)[1]

# 3. Take 15 consecutive hours starting from that 8 o'clock (8:00 - 22:00)
# Assume we want to plot a 15-hour window
plot_slice <- start_index:(start_index + 14)

# 4. Prepare x-axis values for plotting
x_plot <- all_valid_hours[plot_slice]

# Recompute the corresponding y values (Aw curve)
# Note: we need rows from the bs matrix corresponding to the same slice
# The row index here is based on the subset filtered by idx
y_plot <- bs[plot_slice, 1] * fit3$xm["Aw1"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 2] * fit3$xm["Aw2"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 3] * fit3$xm["Aw3"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 4] * fit3$xm["Aw4"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 5] * fit3$xm["Aw5"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 6] * fit3$xm["Aw6"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 7] * fit3$xm["Aw7"] + 
          bs[plot_slice, 8] * fit3$xm["Aw8"]

# 5. Print x-axis to verify it is 8, 9, ... 22
print(x_plot) 

# 6. Plot
plot(x_plot, y_plot, 
     type = 'l', lwd = 2, col = "blue",
     main = "Effective Window Area (Corrected Day Profile)",
     xlab = "Hour of Day", ylab = "Effective Area (m2)")
grid()
```





```{r}
Pred3 <- predict(fit3) 

# 2. Compute residuals

residuals_fit3 <- Pred3[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit3 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with 8-spines Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```
comment: Aw1 and Aw2 always hit the bounds (not credible). Aw7 and Aw8 correspond to time periods with no sun, yet they increase (not credible). The residuals show basically no improvement.
Following the hint, below we modify the equations so that most solar heat goes into the thermal mass and only a small part heats the air, hoping to avoid Aw blowing up in the morning.
Now build a new fit4.

```{r}
sdeTiTm4 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  # --- 1. Build model ---
  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 2. Define the solar spline sum (for cleanliness you could define it as a string, but we write it directly in the formulas here) ---
  # Solar term = (Aw1*bs1 + ... + Aw8*bs8) * Gv

  # --- 3. Modify system equations (core change) ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # Add p_solar. Only a p_solar fraction of solar gain directly heats the air.
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      Ph + 
      p_solar * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5 + Aw6*bs6 + Aw7*bs7 + Aw8*bs8) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall/thermal mass): 
  # Add solar gain! Receives the remaining (1-p_solar) fraction.
  # Note: divide by Cm (wall heat capacity)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1-p_solar) * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5 + Aw6*bs6 + Aw7*bs7 + Aw8*bs8) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 4. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, bs6, bs7, bs8)

  # --- 5. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 6. Parameter setup ---
  # Initial states
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 1,    lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init = 1000, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 20,   lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20,   lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))

  # 🆕 New parameter: p_solar (solar allocation ratio)
  # Initial guess 0.2 (20% to air, 80% to wall), range 0 to 1
  model$setParameter(p_solar = c(init = 0.2, lb = 0, ub = 1))

  # Noise parameters
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # Aw spline coefficients (keep ub=25 limit)
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw6 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw7 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw8 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # --- 7. Run optimization ---
  fit = model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit4 <- sdeTiTm4(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1)
summary(fit4)
```

The output tells me p_solar = 0.99853, meaning almost all solar gain is allocated to the air. That is clearly nonsense. Next we will forcibly intervene and send (almost) all solar gain to the wall by setting p_solar ≈ 0.

```{r}
sdeTiTm5 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  # --- 1. Build model ---
  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 2. Define the solar spline sum (for cleanliness you could define it as a string, but we write it directly in the formulas here) ---
  # Solar term = (Aw1*bs1 + ... + Aw8*bs8) * Gv

  # --- 3. Modify system equations (core change) ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # Add p_solar. Only a p_solar fraction of solar gain directly heats the air.
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      Ph + 
      p_solar * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5 + Aw6*bs6 + Aw7*bs7 + Aw8*bs8) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall/thermal mass): 
  # Add solar gain! Receives the remaining (1-p_solar) fraction.
  # Note: divide by Cm (wall heat capacity)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1-p_solar) * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5 + Aw6*bs6 + Aw7*bs7 + Aw8*bs8) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 4. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, bs6, bs7, bs8)

  # --- 5. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 6. Parameter setup ---
  # Initial states
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 1,    lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init = 1000, lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 20,   lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20,   lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))

  # 🆕 New parameter: p_solar (solar allocation ratio)
  # Initial guess 0.2 (20% to air, 80% to wall), range 0 to 1
  model$setParameter(p_solar = c(init=0.0001, lb=0, ub=0.001))

  # Noise parameters
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # Aw spline coefficients (keep ub=25 limit)
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw6 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw7 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw8 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # --- 7. Run optimization ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit5 <- sdeTiTm5(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1)
summary(fit5)
```

```{r}
Pred5 <- predict(fit5) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit5 <- Pred5[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit5 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```

Now we restrict solar heat to go entirely into the wall, and the wall cannot handle it (it becomes overly sensitive). We can see the wall heat capacity Cm "collapses": previously Cm was about 85,000 (a thick concrete wall), now it becomes 60, i.e., a paper-thin wall that loses its heat storage capability—heats up instantly under sun and cools instantly when sun is gone.
In addition, the solar parameters disappear: Aw3–Aw8 all become 0. The model "discovers" that if the wall (Cm) becomes so small, then as soon as there is any sun (Aw > 0) shining on it, the wall temperature (Tm) will explode upwards instantly.
To prevent temperature from blowing up, the optimizer simply closes the window during the day (Aw=0), refusing solar heat input. In order to make Tm quickly warm up and heat Ti, the model is forced to shrink Cm by a factor of ~1000 (from ~80k to 60). As a result, Tm loses its "thermal inertia" and becomes extremely reactive.


Next, we will force sunlight to hit the wall, and the wall must remain a thick wall!

```{r}
sdeTiTm6 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  # --- 1. Build model ---
  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 2. Define the solar spline sum (for cleanliness you could define it as a string, but we write it directly in the formulas here) ---
  # Solar term = (Aw1*bs1 + ... + Aw8*bs8) * Gv

  # --- 3. Modify system equations (core change) ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # Add p_solar. Only a p_solar fraction of solar gain directly heats the air.
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      Ph + 
      p_solar * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5 + Aw6*bs6 + Aw7*bs7 + Aw8*bs8) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall/thermal mass): 
  # Add solar gain! Receives the remaining (1-p_solar) fraction.
  # Note: divide by Cm (wall heat capacity)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1-p_solar) * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5 + Aw6*bs6 + Aw7*bs7 + Aw8*bs8) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 4. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, bs6, bs7, bs8)

  # --- 5. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 6. Parameter setup ---
  # Initial states
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 1,    lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm = c(init=85000, lb=50000, ub=150000))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12, lb=10, ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20,   lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))

  # 🆕 New parameter: p_solar (solar allocation ratio)
  # Initial guess 0.2 (20% to air, 80% to wall), range 0 to 1
  model$setParameter(p_solar = c(init=0.1, lb=0, ub=0.2))

  # Noise parameters
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # Aw spline coefficients (keep ub=25 limit)
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw6 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw7 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw8 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # --- 7. Run optimization ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit6 <- sdeTiTm6(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1)
summary(fit6)
```


```{r}
Pred6 <- predict(fit6) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit6 <- Pred6[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit6 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```
Could it be that df is too large and causes multicollinearity? Here I will try df=5 again with the same environment settings.

```{r}
library(splines)
idx <- (Hour>8 & Hour < 23) # It is impossible to fit a window area for the hours without any sun, so we limit the window area estimation to the hours with sun.
bs = bs(Hour[idx],df=5,intercept=TRUE) 

# What does the splines look like?
plot(bs[14:27,1],type='l')
lines(bs[ 14:27,2])
lines(bs[ 14:27,3])
lines(bs[ 14:27,4])
lines(bs[ 14:27,5])

bs1 <- bs2 <- bs3 <- bs4 <- bs5 <- bs6 <- numeric(dim(AllDat)[1])

bs1[idx] = bs[,1]
bs2[idx] = bs[,2]
bs3[idx] = bs[,3]
bs4[idx] = bs[,4]
bs5[idx] = bs[,5]

AllDat$bs1 = bs1
AllDat$bs2 = bs2
AllDat$bs3 = bs3
AllDat$bs4 = bs4
AllDat$bs5 = bs5
```


```{r}
sdeTiTm7 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  # --- 1. Build model ---
  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 2. Define the solar spline sum (for cleanliness you could define it as a string, but we write it directly in the formulas here) ---
  # Solar term = (Aw1*bs1 + ... + Aw8*bs8) * Gv

  # --- 3. Modify system equations (core change) ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # Add p_solar. Only a p_solar fraction of solar gain directly heats the air.
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      Ph + 
      p_solar * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall/thermal mass): 
  # Add solar gain! Receives the remaining (1-p_solar) fraction.
  # Note: divide by Cm (wall heat capacity)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1-p_solar) * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 4. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5)

  # --- 5. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 6. Parameter setup ---
  # Initial states
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 1,    lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm = c(init=85000, lb=50000, ub=150000))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12, lb=10, ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 20,   lb = 1E-4, ub = 1E5))

  # 🆕 New parameter: p_solar (solar allocation ratio)
  # Initial guess 0.2 (20% to air, 80% to wall), range 0 to 1
  model$setParameter(p_solar = c(init=0.5, lb=0.1, ub=0.9))

  # Noise parameters
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # Aw spline coefficients (keep ub=25 limit)
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))


  # --- 7. Run optimization ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```

```{r}
fit7 <- sdeTiTm7(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1)
summary(fit7)
```


```{r}
Pred7 <- predict(fit7) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit7 <- Pred7[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit7 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```
No change—exit with regrets.
Next, we assume that around 9–10am students entering class introduces a systematic bias; let's account for that.

```{r}
# 1. Make sure Hour exists (if not defined previously)
Hour <- as.numeric(strftime(AllDat$date, format="%H"))

# 2. Define student presence time: 9:00 to 16:00 (including 16)
# i.e., 09:00 - 16:59 is considered occupied
AllDat$Occupancy <- ifelse(Hour >= 10 & Hour <= 16, 1, 0)

# 3. Check how many points are marked as 1
table(AllDat$Occupancy)
# You should see a batch of points set to 1
```

```{r}
sdeTiTm8 <- function(data, yTi, Ph, Occupancy){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph
  data$Occupancy <- Occupancy # store in data

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- System equations ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # Add term: + Q_occ * Occupancy
  # Only effective when Occupancy=1 (9am-4pm)
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      Ph + 
      Q_occ * Occupancy +  # <--- extra heat from students (kW)
      p_solar * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall): 
  # Receives most solar gain (1-p_solar)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1-p_solar) * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- Inputs ---
  # Remember to include Occupancy
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, Occupancy) 

  # --- Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- Parameter setup ---
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters (use the reasonable ranges you validated before)
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 8.5,  lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))

  # ⚠️ Key: force Cm to be large (thermal mass) to prevent collapse
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init=130000, lb=50000, ub=200000)) 

  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12, lb=8, ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init=0.5, lb=0.1, ub=10))

  # 🆕 New parameter: Q_occ (student heat power)
  # Range set to -2 to 10 kW. Allowing negative is to avoid some counterintuitive situations (e.g., ventilation losses exceeding body heat).
  model$setParameter(Q_occ = c(init = 1, lb = -2, ub = 10))

  # Solar allocation p_solar (allow wide range)
  model$setParameter(p_solar = c(init=0.5, lb=0, ub=0.99))

  # Spline coefficients (limit to 25)
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # Noise
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # --- Run ---
  # Use capture.output to hide verbose optimization output
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
# Run the model
fit8 <- sdeTiTm8(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1, AllDat$Occupancy)

# View results
summary(fit8)
```

```{r}
Pred8 <- predict(fit8) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit8 <- Pred8[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit8 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```


Next, we will not split solar heat into the thermal mass. Instead, we split heater power into air and thermal mass, and try again:

```{r}
sdeTiTm9 <- function(data, yTi, Ph, Occupancy){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph
  data$Occupancy <- Occupancy

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # - Solar gain (Aw*Gv): all stays here (default)
  # - Heater (Ph): multiplied by p_ph (only a fraction directly heats air)
  # - Student heat (Q_occ): keep it
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph +  # <--- change: split heater
      Q_occ * Occupancy + 
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv # <--- change: solar gain fully back to air
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall/thermal mass): 
  # - Heater (Ph): receives the remaining (1-p_ph)
  # - Solar gain: removed
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph # <--- change: remaining heater goes into wall
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, Occupancy)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup ---
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 8.5,  lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  # Still recommend locking Cm because it now takes the heater's heat storage role
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init=130000, lb=50000, ub=200000)) 
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12, lb=8, ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init=0.5, lb=0.1, ub=10))

  # Student heat
  model$setParameter(Q_occ = c(init = 1, lb = -2, ub = 10))

  # 🆕 New parameter: p_ph (heater allocation ratio)
  # Range 0-1.
  # Guess: radiators mostly heat the air, but there is delay.
  # init=0.5: assume 50% fast heating, 50% slow heating
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init=0.5, lb=0, ub=1))

  # Solar parameters (Aw)
  # Note: since solar is all to air now, Aw may want to hit bounds again when Gv is weak in the morning
  # Keep ub=25
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # Noise
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # --- Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit9 <- sdeTiTm9(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1, AllDat$Occupancy)

# View results
summary(fit9)
```



```{r}
Pred9 <- predict(fit9) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit9 <- Pred9[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit9 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```

```{r}
# Compute RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error) - measures the magnitude of prediction error
rmse <- sqrt(mean(residuals_fit9^2, na.rm = TRUE))

# Compute MAE (Mean Absolute Error) - reflects average deviation
mae <- mean(abs(residuals_fit9), na.rm = TRUE)

# ========================================================
# 6. Model selection metrics (AIC, BIC)
# ========================================================

# Extract information from the fit10 object
nll <- fit10$loglik      # Negative Log-Likelihood
k   <- length(fit9$xm)  # Number of model parameters
n   <- length(na.omit(residuals_fit9)) # Number of effective observations

# Compute AIC and BIC (lower is better)
aic_val <- 2 * k + 2 * nll
bic_val <- log(n) * k + 2 * nll

# ========================================================
# 7. Print evaluation report
# ========================================================
cat("\n--------------------------------------------\n")
cat("       Model fit9 Evaluation Report       \n")
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
cat(sprintf("RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error): %.4f °C\n", rmse))
cat(sprintf("MAE  (Mean Absolute Error)    : %.4f °C\n", mae))
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
cat(sprintf("AIC  (Akaike Information Criterion)  : %.2f\n", aic_val))
cat(sprintf("BIC  (Bayesian Information Criterion): %.2f\n", bic_val))
cat(sprintf("LogLikelihood                  : %.2f\n", -nll)) # Note: LogLikelihood is often displayed as a negative number
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")

# ========================================================
# 8. Autocorrelation plot (ACF) - check for remaining structure
# ========================================================
# Open a new window or plot below the original figure
# If you want to see residuals and ACF in the same figure, uncomment the line below
# par(mfrow=c(2,1)) 

# Plot ACF
acf(residuals_fit9, 
    lag.max = 24, # Check correlation over the past 24 hours
    na.action = na.pass, 
    main = "ACF of Residuals (fit9)")

```





Add student impact into the diffusion term

```{r}
sdeTiTm10 <- function(data, yTi, Ph, Occupancy){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph
  data$Occupancy <- Occupancy

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # - Solar gain (Aw*Gv): all stays here (default)
  # - Heater (Ph): multiplied by p_ph (only a fraction directly heats air)
  # - Student heat (Q_occ): keep it
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph +  # <--- change: split heater
      Q_occ * Occupancy + 
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv # <--- change: solar gain fully back to air
    )*dt + exp(p11 + p_stu_noise * Occupancy)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall/thermal mass): 
  # - Heater (Ph): receives the remaining (1-p_ph)
  # - Solar gain: removed
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph # <--- change: remaining heater goes into wall
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, Occupancy)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup ---
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 8.5,  lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  # Still recommend locking Cm because it now takes the heater's heat storage role
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init=130000, lb=50000, ub=200000)) 
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12, lb=8, ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init=0.5, lb=0.1, ub=10))

  # Student heat
  model$setParameter(Q_occ = c(init = 1, lb = -2, ub = 10))

  # 🆕 New parameter: p_ph (heater allocation ratio)
  # Range 0-1.
  # Guess: radiators mostly heat the air, but there is delay.
  # init=0.5: assume 50% fast heating, 50% slow heating
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init=0.5, lb=0, ub=1))

  # Solar parameters (Aw)
  # Note: since solar is all to air now, Aw may want to hit bounds again when Gv is weak in the morning
  # Keep ub=25
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  # Set init to 0 (assume no effect), allow both positive and negative
  model$setParameter(p_stu_noise = c(init = 0, lb = -5, ub = 5))

  # Noise
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # --- Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit10 <- sdeTiTm10(AllDat, AllDat$yTi1, AllDat$Ph1, AllDat$Occupancy)

# View results
summary(fit10)
```





```{r}
Pred10 <- predict(fit10) # Assume your new model is called fit2

# 2. Compute residuals
# Note: Pred2[[1]] corresponds to the first data segment
residuals_fit10 <- Pred10[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - AllDat$yTi1

# 3. Plot (compare by Hour)
plot(residuals_fit10 ~ Hour, 
     main = "Residuals with Time-Varying Aw", 
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix axis limits to make comparisons with previous plots easier

# 4. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```

```{r}
# ========================================================
# 5. Quantitative metrics (RMSE, MAE)
# ========================================================

# Compute RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error) - measures the magnitude of prediction error
rmse <- sqrt(mean(residuals_fit10^2, na.rm = TRUE))

# Compute MAE (Mean Absolute Error) - reflects average deviation
mae <- mean(abs(residuals_fit10), na.rm = TRUE)

# ========================================================
# 6. Model selection metrics (AIC, BIC)
# ========================================================

# Extract information from the fit10 object
nll <- fit10$loglik      # Negative Log-Likelihood
k   <- length(fit10$xm)  # Number of model parameters
n   <- length(na.omit(residuals_fit10)) # Number of effective observations

# Compute AIC and BIC (lower is better)
aic_val <- 2 * k + 2 * nll
bic_val <- log(n) * k + 2 * nll

# ========================================================
# 7. Print evaluation report
# ========================================================
cat("\n--------------------------------------------\n")
cat("       Model fit10 Evaluation Report       \n")
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
cat(sprintf("RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error): %.4f °C\n", rmse))
cat(sprintf("MAE  (Mean Absolute Error)    : %.4f °C\n", mae))
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
cat(sprintf("AIC  (Akaike Information Criterion)  : %.2f\n", aic_val))
cat(sprintf("BIC  (Bayesian Information Criterion): %.2f\n", bic_val))
cat(sprintf("LogLikelihood                  : %.2f\n", -nll)) # Convert back to positive for display
cat("--------------------------------------------\n")

# ========================================================
# 8. Autocorrelation plot (ACF) - check for remaining structure
# ========================================================
# Open a new window or plot below the original figure
# If you want to see residuals and ACF in the same figure, uncomment the line below
# par(mfrow=c(2,1)) 

# Plot ACF
acf(residuals_fit10, 
    lag.max = 24, # Check correlation over the past 24 hours
    na.action = na.pass, 
    main = "ACF of Residuals (fit10)")

# Restore plotting layout
# par(mfrow=c(1,1))
```

```{r}
# ========================================================
# ========================================================
Pred10 <- predict(fit10)
y_pred <- Pred10[[1]]$state$pred$Ti
y_obs  <- AllDat$yTi1
time_axis <- AllDat$date

# Subset: Feb 6, 2015
start_time <- as.POSIXct("2015-02-06 00:00:00", tz="UTC")
end_time   <- as.POSIXct("2015-02-06 23:59:59", tz="UTC")
idx_plot   <- which(time_axis >= start_time & time_axis <= end_time)

# ========================================================
# 2. Plot (Blue Line vs Red Line)
# ========================================================
# Plot observations -> thick blue line
plot(time_axis[idx_plot], y_obs[idx_plot], 
     type = 'l',           # l = line
     lwd = 3,              # line width
     col = "blue",         # blue
     xlab = "Date", 
     ylab = "Temperature (°C)",
     main = "Model Prediction vs. Observation (Feb 1 - Feb 7)",
     ylim = c(min(y_obs[idx_plot]), max(y_obs[idx_plot])))

# Plot predictions -> thinner red line
lines(time_axis[idx_plot], y_pred[idx_plot], 
      col = "red",         # red
      lwd = 2,             # thinner
      lty = 1)             # solid line

# ========================================================
# 3. Add legend and grid
# ========================================================
grid() # add grid

legend("topright", 
       legend = c("Observation (Real)", "Prediction (Model)"),
       col = c("blue", "red"),
       lty = c(1, 1),      # both solid
       lwd = c(3, 2),      # match line widths
       bg = "white")       # white background
```

```{r}
# 1. Ensure time format is correct (assume timezone UTC; adjust if needed)
AllDat$date <- as.POSIXct(AllDat$date, tz="UTC") 

# 2. Define time range (2015-01-13 to 2015-03-25)
start_date <- as.POSIXct("2015-01-13 00:00:00", tz="UTC")
end_date   <- as.POSIXct("2015-03-25 23:59:59", tz="UTC")

# 3. Extract indices
idx_subset <- which(AllDat$date >= start_date & AllDat$date <= end_date)

# 4. Create subset dataset (includes all columns: yTi1, Ph1, bs1-bs5, etc.)
Data_Subset <- AllDat[idx_subset, ]

# Check data size
print(paste("Selected rows:", nrow(Data_Subset)))
```



```{r}
sdeTiTm11 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){ # <--- Occupancy argument removed
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # - Solar gain: all here
  # - Heater: p_ph fraction here
  # - ❌ Removed Q_occ * Occupancy
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph +  # keep heater split
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall): 
  # - Heater: (1-p_ph) fraction here
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  # ❌ Removed Occupancy
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup ---
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 8.5,  lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  # Lock Cm to prevent collapse (based on prior experience)
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init=130000, lb=50000, ub=200000)) 
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12, lb=8, ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init=0.5, lb=0.1, ub=10))

  # ❌ Removed Q_occ parameter setting

  # Heater allocation ratio
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init=0.5, lb=0, ub=1))

  # Solar parameters (Aw) - limit ub=25
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # Noise
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # --- Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
# Run the model
fit11 <- sdeTiTm11(Data_Subset, Data_Subset$yTi1, Data_Subset$Ph1)

# View results
summary(fit11)
```


```{r}
# ========================================================
# 1. Generate predictions
# ========================================================
Pred11 <- predict(fit11) 

# fit11 only contains data from Jan 13 to Mar 25
residuals_fit11 <- Pred11[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - Data_Subset$yTi1

# ========================================================
# 3. Prepare time axis for plotting
# ========================================================
# Hour must be re-extracted from Data_Subset
# Otherwise the x-axis will not align
Hour_subset <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Subset$date, format="%H"))

# ========================================================
# 4. Plot (compare by Hour)
# ========================================================
plot(residuals_fit11 ~ Hour_subset, 
     main = "Residuals (Jan 13 - Mar 25)", 
     xlab = "Hour of Day",
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix y-axis limits for comparison

# 5. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```


Generate occupancy (student) splines
```{r}
library(splines)

# 1. Ensure time format is correct and subset (you already did this; just double-check)
AllDat$date <- as.POSIXct(AllDat$date, tz="UTC") 
start_date <- as.POSIXct("2015-01-13 00:00:00", tz="UTC")
end_date   <- as.POSIXct("2015-03-25 23:59:59", tz="UTC")
idx_subset <- which(AllDat$date >= start_date & AllDat$date <= end_date)
Data_Subset <- AllDat[idx_subset, ]

# 2. Extract hours
Hour_subset <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Subset$date, format="%H"))

# 3. Define student presence time window (9:00 - 16:00)
idx_occ_time <- (Hour_subset >= 9 & Hour_subset <= 16)

# 4. Build splines for student heat (df=5)
# These splines only describe variation within 9:00-16:00
obs <- bs(Hour_subset[idx_occ_time], df=5, intercept=TRUE)

# 5. Initialize all-zero vectors
obs1 <- obs2 <- obs3 <- obs4 <- obs5 <- numeric(nrow(Data_Subset))

# 6. Fill values
obs1[idx_occ_time] = obs[,1]
obs2[idx_occ_time] = obs[,2]
obs3[idx_occ_time] = obs[,3]
obs4[idx_occ_time] = obs[,4]
obs5[idx_occ_time] = obs[,5]

# 7. Store in Data_Subset (note: store in this subset, not in AllDat)
Data_Subset$obs1 = obs1
Data_Subset$obs2 = obs2
Data_Subset$obs3 = obs3
Data_Subset$obs4 = obs4
Data_Subset$obs5 = obs5

# Check
print(paste("Selected rows:", nrow(Data_Subset)))
```

```{r}
sdeTiTm12 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph + 
      # 🔥 Time-varying student heat (5 parameters)
      # This is a time-varying power curve, replacing the old Q_occ * Occupancy
      (Q1*obs1 + Q2*obs2 + Q3*obs3 + Q4*obs4 + Q5*obs5) + 
      # Solar splines (keep as-is)
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm unchanged (receives remaining heater power)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  # Remember to include obs1-obs5
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, obs1, obs2, obs3, obs4, obs5)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup ---
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters (lock Cm to prevent collapse)
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=10.0, lb=9.9, ub=10.1)) 
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init=52000, lb=51000, ub=53000))
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init=7.0, lb=6.9, ub=7.1))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init=0.42, lb=0.41, ub=0.43))

  # 🆕 Student heat spline coefficients (Q1 - Q5)
  # Allow negative values (-2 to 10 kW), just in case
  model$setParameter(Q1 = c(init=1, lb=-2, ub=10)) # morning
  model$setParameter(Q2 = c(init=2, lb=-2, ub=10)) # late morning peak
  model$setParameter(Q3 = c(init=1, lb=-2, ub=10)) # lunch
  model$setParameter(Q4 = c(init=2, lb=-2, ub=10)) # afternoon
  model$setParameter(Q5 = c(init=1, lb=-2, ub=10)) # after school

  # Heater allocation
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init=0.7, lb=0, ub=1))

  # Solar parameters (ub=25)
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # Noise
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # --- 5. Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```



```{r}
# 1. Run the model (pass Data_Subset)
fit12 <- sdeTiTm12(Data_Subset, Data_Subset$yTi1, Data_Subset$Ph1)

# 2. View parameters
summary(fit12)
```


```{r}
Pred12 <- predict(fit12) 

# fit11 only contains data from Jan 13 to Mar 25
residuals_fit12 <- Pred12[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - Data_Subset$yTi1

# ========================================================
# 3. Prepare time axis for plotting
# ========================================================
# Hour must be re-extracted from Data_Subset
# Otherwise the x-axis will not align
Hour_subset <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Subset$date, format="%H"))

# ========================================================
# 4. Plot (compare by Hour)
# ========================================================
plot(residuals_fit12 ~ Hour_subset, 
     main = "Residuals (Jan 13 - Mar 25)", 
     xlab = "Hour of Day",
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix y-axis limits for comparison

# 5. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```
It improves a bit, but still not good enough. Next we will use weekend data to estimate Aw, then lock it.

```{r}
# 1. Ensure date in Data_Subset is time format
# (If you've already run the subsetting code, this is usually done.)
Data_Subset$date <- as.POSIXct(Data_Subset$date, tz="UTC")

# 2. Compute weekday
# format(..., "%u") returns: 1=Mon, ..., 6=Sat, 7=Sun
Data_Subset$weekday_num <- as.numeric(format(Data_Subset$date, "%u"))

# 3. Select weekends (>=6: Saturday and Sunday)
Data_Weekend <- subset(Data_Subset, weekday_num >= 6)

# --- Verify ---
# Check whether it's really only Saturday and Sunday
print(unique(format(Data_Weekend$date, "%Y-%m-%d %A")))
# Check how many rows were selected
print(paste("Weekend rows:", nrow(Data_Weekend)))
```

```{r}
# 1. Ensure date in Data_Subset is time format
sdeTiTm13 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){ 
  # No need for Occupancy since weekends are assumed unoccupied
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # - Solar gain: all here (to capture fast solar response)
  # - Heater: p_ph fraction here
  # - Students: none
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph + 
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall): 
  # - Heater: (1-p_ph) fraction here (thermal storage)
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  # Make sure data contains bs1-bs5
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup ---
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # Physical parameters (robust settings)
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 8.5,  lb = 1E-5, ub = 1E5))
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init=130000, lb=50000, ub=200000)) # lock thick wall
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init=12,     lb=8,     ub=20))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init=0.5,    lb=0.1,   ub=10))

  # Heater allocation
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init=0.5, lb=0, ub=1))

  # Solar parameters (Aw) - if hitting bounds is severe, increase ub to 30
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init=5, lb=0, ub=25))

  # Noise
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = 1,  lb = -30, ub = 10))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -1, lb = -50, ub = 10))

  # --- Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```



```{r}
# Run weekend model
fit13 <- sdeTiTm13(Data_Weekend, Data_Weekend$yTi1, Data_Weekend$Ph1)

# View results
summary(fit13)
```



```{r}
# ========================================================
# 1. Generate predictions from weekend model
# ========================================================
Pred13 <- predict(fit13)

# ========================================================
# 2. Compute residuals (key: subtract weekend observations)
# ========================================================
# Extract predicted Ti
y_pred_weekend <- Pred13[[1]]$state$pred$Ti

# Compute residuals
residuals_13 <- y_pred_weekend - Data_Weekend$yTi1

# ========================================================
# 3. Extract weekend hours (for x-axis)
# ========================================================
Hour_weekend <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Weekend$date, format="%H"))

# ========================================================
# 4. Plot: residuals vs hour (diagnose diurnal pattern)
# ========================================================
plot(residuals_13 ~ Hour_weekend, 
     main = "Residuals on Weekends (No Students)", 
     xlab = "Hour of Day",
     ylab = "Prediction Error (°C)",
     col = "darkblue",
     pch = 1,
     ylim = c(-2, 2))

# Add 0 reference line
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)

# Add grid
grid()
```


Next, lock the solar parameters!
```{r}
sdeTiTm14 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  # Note: no need to pass Occupancy because obs1-obs5 are already in data
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph + 
      # 🔥 Time-varying student heat (open estimation)
      (Q1*obs1 + Q2*obs2 + Q3*obs3 + Q4*obs4 + Q5*obs5) + 
      # 🔒 Solar splines (parameters will be locked)
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  # Ensure obs1-obs5 (students) and bs1-bs5 (solar) are included
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, obs1, obs2, obs3, obs4, obs5)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup (key step!) ---

  # Initial states (wide range)
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # 🔒 [Physical parameters]: fill in weekend model results and lock them (very small tolerance)
  # Ci = 12.74
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 12.74, lb = 12.73, ub = 12.75))
  # Cm = 163180
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init = 163180, lb = 163170, ub = 163190))
  # Ria = 19.75
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 19.75, lb = 19.74, ub = 19.76))
  # Rim = 0.46
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 0.465, lb = 0.46, ub = 0.47))
  # p_ph = 0.987 (heater almost entirely heats air)
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init = 0.987, lb = 0.98, ub = 1.0))

  # 🔒 [Solar parameters]: fill in weekend model results and lock them
  # Aw1 = 3.53
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init = 3.53, lb = 3.52, ub = 3.54))
  # Aw2 = 4.96
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init = 4.96, lb = 4.95, ub = 4.97))
  # Aw3 ≈ 0 (1.1e-12) -> set to 0
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init = 0, lb = -0.01, ub = 0.01))
  # Aw4 = 9.38
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init = 9.38, lb = 9.37, ub = 9.39))
  # Aw5 ≈ 0 (7.3e-12) -> set to 0
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init = 0, lb = -0.01, ub = 0.01))

  # 🔓 [Student heat parameters]: only these remain open
  model$setParameter(Q1 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 09:00
  model$setParameter(Q2 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 10:30
  model$setParameter(Q3 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 12:00
  model$setParameter(Q4 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 13:30
  model$setParameter(Q5 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 15:00

  # Noise parameters (can be loosened, or kept)
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = -9.7, lb = -20, ub = 0))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = -1.4, lb = -20, ub = 0))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -5.6, lb = -20, ub = 0))

  # --- 5. Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```



```{r}
# 1. Run the final model
fit14 <- sdeTiTm14(Data_Subset, Data_Subset$yTi1, Data_Subset$Ph1)

# 2. View results
summary(fit14)
```


```{r}
Pred14 <- predict(fit14) 

# fit11 only contains data from Jan 13 to Mar 25
residuals_fit14 <- Pred14[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - Data_Subset$yTi1

# ========================================================
# 3. Prepare time axis for plotting
# ========================================================
Hour_subset <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Subset$date, format="%H"))

# ========================================================
# 4. Plot (compare by Hour)
# ========================================================
plot(residuals_fit14 ~ Hour_subset, 
     main = "Residuals", 
     xlab = "Hour of Day",
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix y-axis limits for comparison

# 5. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```

```{r}
# ========================================================
# 5. Numeric evaluation metrics (Metrics Calculation)
# ========================================================

# Remove potential NA values (ctsm usually handles well, but it's safer for statistics)
res_clean <- na.omit(residuals_fit14)

# --- A. Error metrics (lower is better) ---

# 1. MSE (Mean Squared Error)
# Meaning: average of squared errors (the metric you specifically asked for)
mse <- mean(res_clean^2)

# 2. RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error)
# Meaning: sqrt(MSE). Same units as temperature (°C), more interpretable than MSE
rmse <- sqrt(mse)

# 3. MAE (Mean Absolute Error)
# Meaning: average absolute deviation in degrees
mae <- mean(abs(res_clean))

# --- B. Model selection metrics (lower is better) ---

# Extract key information from fit14
nll <- fit14$loglik       # Negative Log-Likelihood
k   <- length(fit14$xm)   # Number of parameters used
n   <- length(res_clean)  # Sample size

# 4. AIC (Akaike Information Criterion)
aic_val <- 2 * k + 2 * nll

# 5. BIC (Bayesian Information Criterion)
bic_val <- log(n) * k + 2 * nll

# ========================================================
# 6. Print final report
# ========================================================
cat("\n===========================================\n")
cat("       Model fit14 Final Evaluation Report       \n")
cat("===========================================\n")
cat("--- Prediction Accuracy (Error Metrics) ---\n")
cat(sprintf("MSE  (Mean Squared Error) : %.4f\n", mse))
cat(sprintf("RMSE (Root MSE)           : %.4f °C\n", rmse))
cat(sprintf("MAE  (Mean Absolute Error): %.4f °C\n", mae))
cat("\n")
cat("--- Model Fit / Selection ---\n")
cat(sprintf("AIC                        : %.2f\n", aic_val))
cat(sprintf("BIC                        : %.2f\n", bic_val))
cat(sprintf("Log-Likelihood             : %.2f\n", -nll)) # Convert back to positive for display
cat("===========================================\n")

# ========================================================
# 7. (Optional) Residual autocorrelation check (ACF)
# ========================================================
# Check whether the model still misses any structure
acf(res_clean, lag.max = 24, main = "ACF of Residuals (fit14)")
```


Does adding p_solar better match the physics?
```{r}
sdeTiTm15 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  # Input data columns
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---

  # dTi (air): 
  # - Solar gain: now receives only p_solar fraction
  # - Heater: p_ph fraction
  # - Students: full Q (dynamic splines)
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph + 
      (Q1*obs1 + Q2*obs2 + Q3*obs3 + Q4*obs4 + Q5*obs5) + 
      # 🔥 Change: multiply by p_solar
      p_solar * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  # dTm (wall): 
  # - Heater: (1-p_ph) fraction
  # - Solar gain: receives remaining (1-p_solar) fraction
  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph +
      # 🔥 Change: add solar term (1-p_solar)
      (1 - p_solar) * (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, obs1, obs2, obs3, obs4, obs5)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup ---

  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # 🔒 [Physical parameters] (keep locked)
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 12.74, lb = 12.73, ub = 12.75))
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init = 163180, lb = 163170, ub = 163190))
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 19.75, lb = 19.74, ub = 19.76))
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 0.465, lb = 0.46, ub = 0.47))
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init = 0.987, lb = 0.98, ub = 1.0))

  # 🆕 [New parameter]: p_solar (solar allocation ratio)
  # Range 0-1. Typically most goes into wall (small p), but it could be otherwise.
  model$setParameter(p_solar = c(init = 0.2, lb = 0, ub = 1))

  # 🔒 [Solar parameters Aw] (keep locked)
  # Note: previously Aw was estimated assuming 100% goes to air.
  # Now the structure changes; these values may be slightly "mismatched", but it's OK as a first attempt.
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init = 3.53, lb = 3.52, ub = 3.54))
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init = 4.96, lb = 4.95, ub = 4.97))
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init = 0, lb = -0.01, ub = 0.01))
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init = 9.38, lb = 9.37, ub = 9.39))
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init = 0, lb = -0.01, ub = 0.01))

  # 🔓 [Student heat Q] (open estimation)
  model$setParameter(Q1 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10))
  model$setParameter(Q2 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10))
  model$setParameter(Q3 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10))
  model$setParameter(Q4 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10))
  model$setParameter(Q5 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10))

  # Noise parameters
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = -9.7, lb = -20, ub = 0))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = -1.4, lb = -20, ub = 0))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -5.6, lb = -20, ub = 0))

  # --- 5. Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit15 <- sdeTiTm15(Data_Subset, Data_Subset$yTi1, Data_Subset$Ph1)

# 2. View results
summary(fit15)
```

```{r}
Pred15 <- predict(fit15) 

# fit11 only contains data from Jan 13 to Mar 25
residuals_fit15 <- Pred15[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - Data_Subset$yTi1

# ========================================================
# 3. Prepare time axis for plotting
# ========================================================
Hour_subset <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Subset$date, format="%H"))

# ========================================================
# 4. Plot (compare by Hour)
# ========================================================
plot(residuals_fit15 ~ Hour_subset, 
     main = "Residuals ", 
     xlab = "Hour of Day",
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix y-axis limits for comparison

# 5. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```

```{r}
Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "English")
library(ggplot2)

# Define holiday periods (for background shading)
rects <- data.frame(
  xmin = as.POSIXct(c("2014-12-22", "2015-04-01")),
  xmax = as.POSIXct(c("2015-01-12", "2015-04-30")),
  ymin = -Inf,
  ymax = Inf
)

ggplot(AllDat, aes(x = date, y = yTi1)) +
  # 1. Add holiday shaded regions (semi-transparent red)
  geom_rect(data = rects, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "red", alpha = 0.2) +
  # 2. Plot temperature time series
  geom_line(color = "black", size = 0.3) +
  # 3. Add labels (optional)
  annotate("text", x = as.POSIXct("2015-01-01"), y = 25, label = "Winter Break", color = "red") +
  annotate("text", x = as.POSIXct("2015-04-10"), y = 25, label = "Easter Break", color = "red") +
  # 4. Axes and titles
  labs(title = "Indoor Temperature (Room 1) Over Time",
       subtitle = "Highlighted regions show periods with no students (holidays)",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Temperature (°C)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
```




Next, lock e11 between -6 and -5

```{r}
sdeTiTm16 <- function(data, yTi, Ph){
  # Note: no need to pass Occupancy because obs1-obs5 are already in data
  data$yTi <- yTi
  data$Ph  <- Ph

  model <- ctsm()

  # --- 1. System equations ---
  model$addSystem(dTi ~ 1/Ci * ( 
      1/Ria*(Ta-Ti) + 
      1/Rim*(Tm-Ti) + 
      p_ph * Ph + 
      # 🔥 Time-varying student heat (open estimation)
      (Q1*obs1 + Q2*obs2 + Q3*obs3 + Q4*obs4 + Q5*obs5) + 
      # 🔒 Solar splines (parameters will be locked)
      (Aw1*bs1 + Aw2*bs2 + Aw3*bs3 + Aw4*bs4 + Aw5*bs5) * Gv 
    )*dt + exp(p11)*dw1)

  model$addSystem(dTm ~ 1/Cm * ( 
      1/Rim*(Ti-Tm) + 
      (1 - p_ph) * Ph
    )*dt + exp(p22)*dw2)

  # --- 2. Inputs ---
  # Ensure obs1-obs5 (students) and bs1-bs5 (solar) are included
  model$addInput(Ta, Gv, Ph, bs1, bs2, bs3, bs4, bs5, obs1, obs2, obs3, obs4, obs5)

  # --- 3. Observation equation ---
  model$addObs(yTi ~ Ti)
  model$setVariance(yTi ~ exp(e11))

  # --- 4. Parameter setup (key step!) ---

  # Initial states (wide range)
  model$setParameter(Ti = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))
  model$setParameter(Tm = c(init = 15, lb = 0, ub = 40))

  # 🔒 [Physical parameters]: fill in weekend model results and lock them (very small tolerance)
  # Ci = 12.74
  model$setParameter(Ci  = c(init = 12.74, lb = 12.73, ub = 12.75))
  # Cm = 163180
  model$setParameter(Cm  = c(init = 163180, lb = 163170, ub = 163190))
  # Ria = 19.75
  model$setParameter(Ria = c(init = 19.75, lb = 19.74, ub = 19.76))
  # Rim = 0.46
  model$setParameter(Rim = c(init = 0.465, lb = 0.46, ub = 0.47))
  # p_ph = 0.987 (heater almost entirely heats air)
  model$setParameter(p_ph = c(init = 0.987, lb = 0.98, ub = 1.0))

  # 🔒 [Solar parameters]: fill in weekend model results and lock them
  # Aw1 = 3.53
  model$setParameter(Aw1 = c(init = 3.53, lb = 3.52, ub = 3.54))
  # Aw2 = 4.96
  model$setParameter(Aw2 = c(init = 4.96, lb = 4.95, ub = 4.97))
  # Aw3 ≈ 0 (1.1e-12) -> set to 0
  model$setParameter(Aw3 = c(init = 0, lb = -0.01, ub = 0.01))
  # Aw4 = 9.38
  model$setParameter(Aw4 = c(init = 9.38, lb = 9.37, ub = 9.39))
  # Aw5 ≈ 0 (7.3e-12) -> set to 0
  model$setParameter(Aw5 = c(init = 0, lb = -0.01, ub = 0.01))

  # 🔓 [Student heat parameters]: only these remain open
  model$setParameter(Q1 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 09:00
  model$setParameter(Q2 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 10:30
  model$setParameter(Q3 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 12:00
  model$setParameter(Q4 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 13:30
  model$setParameter(Q5 = c(init=1, lb=-5, ub=10)) # 15:00

  # Noise parameters (can be loosened, or kept)
  model$setParameter(p11 = c(init = -9.7, lb = -20, ub = 0))
  model$setParameter(p22 = c(init = -1.4, lb = -20, ub = 0))
  model$setParameter(e11 = c(init = -5.6, lb = -6, ub = -5))

  # --- 5. Run ---
  capture.output(fit <- model$estimate(data, firstorder = TRUE), file = NULL)
  return(fit)
}
```


```{r}
fit16 <- sdeTiTm16(Data_Subset, Data_Subset$yTi1, Data_Subset$Ph1)

# 2. View results
summary(fit16)
```




```{r}
Pred16 <- predict(fit16) 

# fit11 only contains data from Jan 13 to Mar 25
residuals_fit16 <- Pred15[[1]]$state$pred$Ti - Data_Subset$yTi1

# ========================================================
# 3. Prepare time axis for plotting
# ========================================================
Hour_subset <- as.numeric(strftime(Data_Subset$date, format="%H"))

# ========================================================
# 4. Plot (compare by Hour)
# ========================================================
plot(residuals_fit16 ~ Hour_subset, 
     main = "Residuals ", 
     xlab = "Hour of Day",
     ylab = "Prediction Error (C)",
     ylim = c(-3, 3)) # Fix y-axis limits for comparison

# 5. Add a horizontal 0-line for reference
abline(h=0, col="red", lwd=2)
```



Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
